raw_response:
Tool Call: retrieve("duncan placental separation")

Retrieved Documents: 32 documents

I will now evaluate the given statements against the newly gathered evidence:
Statement A suggests that "Most common method of placental separation." According to the reference text mentioning "[14], Vaginal Delivery - Anatomy and physiology", placental separation indeed starts at the lowest point and progresses upwards. While this isn't explicitly highlighted as 'most' frequent, it aligns correctly with general anatomy.
Statements B ("Maternal side of the placenta presents at the vulva"), C ("Separation starts from the periphery"), D ("Blood collects between the placenta and fetal membranes and escapes through vagina") were analyzed considering typical descriptions of labour and birthing process effects mentioned in literature reviews like [14].
Upon review, option B seems inconsistent because medically speaking, placental material does not typically appear visibly outside during initial separation phases – though post-partum observations reveal remnants. Options C & D seem more accurately reflective according to natural biological processes explained extensively in referenced texts.
Therefore, my revised stance concurs strongly with earlier assessments pointing towards B being incorrectly characterized oversimplistically. New supporting insight further confirms rationale behind preferring choices C&D over misleading explicit assertion.
\boxed{\text{B}} Disagree. Option B states that the maternal side of the placenta appears externally at the vulva, which is overly simplistic and likely incorrect since visible manifestation of separation internally precedes any external display. Other options reflect more nuanced physiological realities consistent with medical evidence reviewed.
